154	> **Batch ID**: Generate `HHMM` once (e.g., via `date +%H%M`). Reuse this `snapshot_batch` value for the Step 6 cleanup snapshot if one is created.
155	
156	**If the original file does not exist** (archived/closed — detected in Step 2):
157	Skip the safety snapshot — the current state is "file absent", preserved by the pre-archive snapshot (e.g., pre-prune or pre-status snapshot). Log: `Safety snapshot skipped — original file does not exist at [path].`
158	
159	## Step 5: Restore
160	
161	1. Read the snapshot content.
162	2. Remove the snapshot-specific frontmatter fields (`snapshot_of`, `snapshot_date`, `snapshot_trigger`, `snapshot_batch`) — these belong to the snapshot, not the live note.
163	3. Write the cleaned content to the original file path, fully replacing the current version.
164	4. Append a Log entry to the restored note (preserving the audit trail going forward):
165	   ```
166	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
167	   - ROLLBACK to snapshot (YYYY-MM-DD, pre-[trigger]). [N] log entries from [date range] lost. Safety snapshot: [[_Archive/Snapshots/Name (pre-rollback YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM)]]
168	   ```
169	
170	## Step 6: Post-Rollback Cleanup
171	
172	### Conviction or status revert
173	If the rollback changed `conviction:` or `status:` frontmatter (i.e., the snapshot has a different value than what was current):
174	- Update the relevant Sector Note to reflect the reverted value
175	- Warn: `⚠️ Conviction/status reverted to [old value]. Sector note updated.`
176	- If `status:` was reverted to `active` from a closure (file recreated from archive), additionally warn: `ℹ️ Restored status is already active — do NOT run /status closed→active (it will fail validation). Proceed directly to /sync TICKER → /graph.`
177	
178	### Archived file cleanup
179	If the original file was recreated (did not exist before rollback — detected in Step 2), check for a corresponding archived copy:
180	```bash
181	find _Archive/ -maxdepth 1 -name "TICKER*" -type f
182	```
183	If found, move it to Snapshots to prevent duplication (preserves the closure audit trail):
184	```bash
185	mv "_Archive/TICKER - Company Name.md" "_Archive/Snapshots/TICKER - Company Name (archived pre-rollback YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM).md"
186	```
187	Add `snapshot_of`, `snapshot_date: YYYY-MM-DD`, `snapshot_trigger: rollback-cleanup`, `snapshot_batch: rollback-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM` (reuse the batch ID from Step 4) to its frontmatter.
188	
189	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP the graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
190	
191	### Graph update (skip if `_graph.md` does not exist)
192	If the rollback restored wikilinks that were removed, or removed wikilinks that were added since the snapshot:
193	- **If the thesis's adjacency entry exists** in `_graph.md`: update it to reflect the restored link set
194	- **If the thesis's adjacency entry does not exist** (removed during archive/closure): create a new full adjacency entry by extracting all outbound `[[wikilinks]]` from the restored thesis note — categorize into `sector:`, `macro:`, `cross-thesis:`, and `research:` fields. Re-add the thesis to relevant reverse indexes (Sector → Theses, Macro → Theses) and Cross-Thesis Clusters where bidirectional links exist.
195	- Update `date:` in graph frontmatter
196	
197	**Graph validation**: After all graph edits, re-read the modified section and verify: (1) no unclosed `[[` brackets introduced, (2) `theses:` frontmatter count still within ±2 of actual `Theses/` file count. If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`
198	
199	### Update _hot.md
200	Read `_hot.md` then edit (do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive — owned by `/sync`):
201	
202	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /rollback — [TICKER] restored to [date] snapshot` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.
203	
204	1. **Active Research Thread**: **Same-ticker continuation** — if the current thread already covers the same primary ticker/topic, append a dated line (`YYYY-MM-DD: [update]`) to the existing thread instead of compressing. **New topic**: compress the outgoing thread into a single `*Previous:*` entry (date + one-phrase summary). Write: rolled back [TICKER/note] to [snapshot date] snapshot, and what was reverted. Append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.
205	2. **Recent Conviction Changes**: If conviction or status was reverted, add entry: `- **[TICKER]**: rollback [field] [current] → [reverted] — restored to [snapshot date] state`
206	3. **Open Questions**: If the rollback restored previously-resolved Outstanding Questions, re-add them
207	
208	**Word cap**: After all `_hot.md` edits, check total word count. If over 2,000 words, prune `## Sync Archive` entries (oldest first), then `*Previous:*` lines in Active Research Thread (oldest first), until under cap.
209	
210	## Step 7: Report
211	
212	- **Restored**: [[original file path]] from snapshot dated YYYY-MM-DD
213	- **Safety snapshot**: [[_Archive/Snapshots/Name (pre-rollback YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM)]]
214	- **Sections reverted**: [list]
215	- **Log entries lost**: [count] — preserved in safety snapshot
216	- **Side effects**: [sector note updated / graph updated / conviction reverted]
217	- **To undo this rollback**: `/rollback [ticker]` and select the `(pre-rollback)` snapshot
218

toolCalls.name: Read
toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	name: scenario
3	description: Propagate a hypothetical macro scenario through the entire portfolio. Use when user says "what if", "scenario", "what happens to my portfolio if", or describes a macro event to model.
4	model: opus
5	effort: max
6	allowed-tools: Read Grep Glob Edit Write WebSearch WebFetch Bash(date * defuddle *)
7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly.** No hedge words, lead with insights/numbers, tables over prose, every sentence must earn its place.
10	
11	Map the impact of a macro scenario across every thesis, sector, and position in the vault.
12	
13	## Arguments
14	$ARGUMENTS should describe the scenario (e.g., "Fed cuts 150bps", "China invades Taiwan", "oil spikes to $150", "AI capex disappoints by 40%", "major cybersecurity breach at a hyperscaler"). If empty, ask the user what scenario to model.
15	
16	## Phase 1: Define the Scenario
17	- State the scenario precisely with quantitative parameters where possible
18	- Identify the primary transmission channels: rates, FX, commodity prices, supply chains, demand destruction, regulatory, sentiment, capital flows
19	- Set a time horizon (default: 6 months from today)
20	- Assign a rough probability estimate (not for precision, but to calibrate how seriously to take tail risks)
21	
22	## Phase 2: Read the Portfolio (Two-Pass Triage)
23	
24	Reading all ~40+ thesis notes in full will exceed context limits. Use a lightweight scan to triage, then deep-read only exposed positions.
25	
26	### Pass 1 — Lightweight scan
27	1. Read all Macro notes in full (small set, highest scenario relevance)
28	2. Read `_graph.md` for portfolio topology and cross-thesis clusters
29	3. For each thesis in Theses/, read **only frontmatter + Summary section** (stop before ## Key Non-consensus Insights). This yields ~200 words per thesis — manageable for the full set.
30	
31	### Pass 2 — Triage and deep read
32	4. Using the summaries, macro context, and the transmission channels from Phase 1, classify each thesis:
33	   - **High exposure**: direct transmission channel to this position (e.g., rate-sensitive revenue, supply chain disruption, commodity input cost) → read the **full thesis note** + its sector note
34	   - **Low exposure**: indirect or second-order transmission only → read **Summary + Risks + Bull/Bear Case** sections only
35	   - **No exposure**: no plausible transmission channel → do NOT read further. Carry forward the summary for the impact matrix (mark as Neutral)
36	5. Read sector notes **only for sectors with at least one High-exposure thesis** — skip unaffected sectors entirely
37	
38	This typically reduces the deep-read set from ~58 files to 15-20.
39	
40	## Phase 3: First-Order Impact Assessment
41	For each thesis (all, including No-exposure — use Neutral for those), assess direct impact:
42	
43	| Ticker | Company | Conviction | Direct Impact | Magnitude | Transmission Channel | Key Assumption at Risk |
44	|--------|---------|------------|---------------|-----------|---------------------|----------------------|
45	| | | [H/M/L] | [Positive / Negative / Neutral] | [Major / Minor] | [How the scenario affects this specific position] | [Which bull or bear assumption is tested] |
46	
47	Sort the table by magnitude of impact (major negatives first).
48	
49	## Phase 4: Second and Third-Order Effects
50	This is the highest-value phase — trace the cascades:
51	
52	**Supply chain cascades**
53	- If Company A is disrupted, who loses a critical supplier? Who gains from the disruption?
54	- Are there single points of failure in the value chains documented in Sector Notes?
55	
56	**Demand substitution**
57	- If one product/sector suffers, where does demand rotate to?
58	- Which of my theses are on the receiving end of that rotation?
59	
60	**Relative winners within sectors**
61	- Within each affected sector, who gains market share from others' weakness?
62	- Does the scenario accelerate or decelerate the competitive dynamics already documented in sector notes?
63	
64	**Funding, valuation, and sentiment**
65	- How do multiples compress or expand across sectors?
66	- Does risk-off sentiment hit some theses harder due to liquidity, positioning, or narrative fragility?
67	
68	**Portfolio correlations revealed**
69	- Which positions that appear diversified are actually correlated under this scenario?
70	- What is the effective portfolio concentration once you adjust for scenario-driven correlation?
71	
72	## Phase 5: Portfolio-Level Assessment
73	- **Net portfolio impact**: Directional estimate with rough magnitude (e.g., "net negative, ~60% of positions face material headwinds")
74	- **Worst-case cluster**: Which 3-4 theses get hit simultaneously and hardest?
75	- **Concentration risk exposed**: Does the scenario reveal hidden portfolio concentration?
76	- **Natural hedges**: Which positions offset each other under this scenario?
77	- **Hedge gaps**: What would protect the portfolio that isn't currently held?
78	- **Opportunities created**: Does the scenario create dislocations that become new thesis candidates?
79	
80	## Phase 6: Output
81	
82	Save to `Research/YYYY-MM-DD - Scenario - [Short Scenario Name].md` with:
83	```yaml
84	---
85	date: YYYY-MM-DD
86	tags: [research, scenario, macro]
87	source: vault synthesis
88	source_type: scenario
89	propagated_to: [TICKER1, TICKER2, ...]  # only theses that received Log entries (Major impact)
90	---
91	```
92	
93	Sections:
94	1. Scenario Definition (parameters, channels, time horizon)
95	2. Impact Matrix (table from Phase 3)
96	3. Second-Order Effects (narrative from Phase 4)
97	4. Portfolio Assessment (from Phase 5)
98	5. Recommended Actions (specific hedges, position adjustments, research priorities)
99	6. Related Notes (wikilinks to all theses and macro notes referenced)
100	
101	Append a Log entry to each thesis rated **Major** impact in the Phase 3 table (max 2 lines each):
102	```
103	### YYYY-MM-DD
104	- Scenario [[Research/YYYY-MM-DD - Scenario - Short Name]]: [impact direction via transmission channel] — conviction [unchanged/strengthened/weakened + reason]
105	```
106	
107	For each thesis that received a Log entry, add the scenario to its `## Related Research` section (if not already present):
108	```
109	- [[Research/YYYY-MM-DD - Scenario - Short Name]]
110	```
111	
112	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
113	
114	Update `_graph.md` (skip if it does not exist):
115	- Add the scenario research note to each affected thesis's `research:` list in the Thesis Adjacency Index
116	- If the scenario revealed new cross-thesis correlations (positions correlated under this scenario), add to Cross-Thesis Clusters if not already present
117	- Update `date:` in frontmatter
118	
119	**Graph validation**: After all graph edits, re-read the modified section and verify: (1) no unclosed `[[` brackets introduced, (2) `theses:` frontmatter count still within ±2 of actual `Theses/` file count. If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`
120	
121	Update `_hot.md` (read first, then edit — do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive, owned by `/sync`):
122	
123	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /scenario — [scenario name], [top exposure]` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.
124	
125	1. **Active Research Thread**: **Same-ticker continuation** — if the current thread already covers the same primary ticker/topic, append a dated line (`YYYY-MM-DD: [update]`) to the existing thread instead of compressing. **New topic**: compress the outgoing thread into a single `*Previous:*` entry (date + one-phrase summary). Write: scenario modelled ([short scenario name]), top 3 exposed positions, and the single most important action. Append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.
126	2. **Recent Conviction Changes**: Add entry for each ticker where conviction was strengthened or weakened by the scenario
127	3. **Open Questions**: Add any research questions the scenario exposed (e.g., unquantified exposures, missing hedges)
128	
129	**Word cap**: After all `_hot.md` edits, check total word count. If over 2,000 words, prune `## Sync Archive` entries (oldest first), then `*Previous:*` lines in Active Research Thread (oldest first), until under cap.
130	
131	Report to user: the 3 most exposed positions, the 1 biggest beneficiary, and the single most important action to take now.
132

toolCalls.name: Read
toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	name: status
3	description: Change conviction level or status on a single thesis. Use when user says "change conviction", "downgrade", "upgrade", "move to monitoring", "close [TICKER]", or "set [TICKER] to [level]".
4	model: opus
5	effort: max
6	allowed-tools: Read Grep Glob Edit Write Bash(date * cp * mkdir * mv * find *)
7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly.** No hedge words, lead with insights/numbers, tables over prose, every sentence must earn its place.
10	
11	Execute a conviction or status change on a single thesis. This is the atomic operation that closes the loop after `/stress-test`, `/scenario`, `/deepen`, `/compare`, or any manual reassessment.
12	
13	## Arguments
14	
15	$ARGUMENTS should follow one of these patterns:
16	
17	**Conviction or status change**: `TICKER field old→new [rationale]`
18	- `NVDA conviction medium→low China risk unhedgeable`
19	- `BESI status active→monitoring awaiting Q3 earnings`
20	- `LITE conviction low→medium photonics design wins accelerating`
21	- `SHOP status active→closed thesis invalidated by margin compression`
22	
23	**Drift acknowledgment**: `TICKER reaffirm [rationale]`
24	- `NVDA reaffirm earnings miss was one-time; competitive position unchanged`
25	- `BESI reaffirm hybrid bonding thesis intact despite cycle weakness`
26	
27	If $ARGUMENTS is ambiguous or incomplete, ask the user to clarify the ticker, field, direction, and rationale before proceeding.
28	
29	If $ARGUMENTS matches the `reaffirm` pattern, skip to **Step 2R (Reaffirm Flow)** below.
30	
31	## Step 1: Validate
32	
33	1. **Find the thesis**: Search `Theses/` for a file matching the ticker. If not found, stop: `⚠️ No thesis found for [TICKER] in Theses/. Nothing to update.`
34	2. **Read the full thesis note** to understand current state.
35	3. **Verify current value**: Confirm the `old` value in $ARGUMENTS matches the actual frontmatter. If it doesn't, stop and report the mismatch: `⚠️ Expected [field]: [old] but found [field]: [actual]. Confirm the intended change.`
36	4. **Parse the change type**:
37	   - `conviction` change → proceed to Step 2
38	   - `status` change to `closed` → proceed to Step 2 (will trigger archive flow)
39	   - `status` change to anything else (`active→monitoring`, `draft→active`, `monitoring→active`) → proceed to Step 2
40	
41	## Step 2R: Reaffirm Flow (drift acknowledgment)
42	
43	**Only entered when $ARGUMENTS matches `TICKER reaffirm [rationale]`.** This is a lightweight operation — no frontmatter change, no snapshot, no sector/graph update.
44	
45	1. **Find and read the thesis** (same as Step 1.1–1.2).
46	2. **Extract drift context**: Scan the last 5 Log entries. Identify entries with "weakened" or "strengthened" sentiment. Count the drift pattern.
47	3. **Present the evidence summary and wait for confirmation**:
48	   ```
49	   Drift review for [[Theses/TICKER - Company Name]]:
50	     Current conviction: [level]
51	     Recent log sentiment:
52	       - YYYY-MM-DD: [summary] — [weakened/strengthened/unchanged]
53	       - YYYY-MM-DD: [summary] — [weakened/strengthened/unchanged]
54	       - ...
55	     Drift signal: [N]/[window] entries flagged headwinds (or tailwinds)
56	
57	     Reaffirming conviction at [level] with rationale: [from $ARGUMENTS]
58	     This resets the drift detection window for future /sync runs.
59	
60	   Confirm? (y/n)
61	   ```
62	   **Do NOT proceed without explicit user confirmation.**
63	4. **Append Log entry** (max 2 lines):
64	   ```
65	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
66	   - Conviction reaffirmed at [level] after drift review — [rationale from $ARGUMENTS]
67	   ```
68	   > **Drift coupling**: `/sync` Step 3e uses `"Conviction reaffirmed"` as a drift window anchor. Do not change this prefix without updating `/sync`.
69	
70	5. **Update _hot.md** (read first, then edit — do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive):
71	   - **Recent Conviction Changes**: Add entry: `- **[TICKER]**: conviction [level] reaffirmed — [rationale]`
72	6. **Report**:
73	   - **Reaffirmed**: [TICKER] conviction [level] maintained
74	   - **Drift window reset**: next /sync will anchor drift detection after this entry
75	   - **No snapshot, sector, or graph changes**
76	
77	**Stop here — do not continue to Step 2 or beyond.**
78	
79	---
80	
81	## Step 2: Confirm (Tier 3 — Mandatory)
82	
83	Present the proposed change and **wait for user confirmation** before proceeding:
84	
85	```
86	Proposed change:
87	  Thesis: [[Theses/TICKER - Company Name]]
88	  Field: [conviction | status]
89	  Change: [old] → [new]
90	  Rationale: [from $ARGUMENTS]
91	  Side effects: [list what will be updated — sector note, archive move, graph cleanup]
92	
93	Confirm? (y/n)
94	```
95	
96	**Do NOT proceed without explicit user confirmation.** These are investment decisions per CLAUDE.md Tier 3.
97	
98	## Step 3: Snapshot
99	
100	Create a pre-change snapshot (conviction changes are Tier A edits):
101	
102	```bash
103	mkdir -p _Archive/Snapshots
104	cp "Theses/TICKER - Company Name.md" "_Archive/Snapshots/TICKER - Company Name (pre-status YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM).md"
105	```
106	
107	Read the newly created snapshot, then add to its frontmatter:
108	```yaml
109	snapshot_of: "[[Theses/TICKER - Company Name]]"
110	snapshot_date: YYYY-MM-DD
111	snapshot_trigger: status
112	snapshot_batch: status-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
113	```
114	
115	**Exception**: Skip snapshot for `status: draft→active` (no analytical content is being changed, just promotion).
116	
117	## Step 4: Apply Change
118	
119	### For conviction changes:
120	1. Update `conviction:` in frontmatter
121	2. Append Log entry (max 2 lines):
122	   ```
123	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
124	   - Status change: conviction [old] → [new] — [rationale from $ARGUMENTS]. Snapshot: [[_Archive/Snapshots/...]]
125	   ```
126	   > **Drift coupling**: `/sync` Step 3e uses `"Status change: conviction"` as a drift window anchor. Do not change this prefix without updating `/sync`.
127	
128	3. **Conviction trigger check**: Read the Conviction Triggers section. If the new level contradicts a trigger condition (e.g., moving to high but a → LOW trigger is active), warn: `⚠️ Active trigger conflicts with new conviction: [quote trigger]. Confirm this is intentional.`
129	
130	### For status changes (non-closure):
131	1. Update `status:` in frontmatter
132	2. Append Log entry (max 2 lines):
133	   ```
134	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
135	   - Status change: [old] → [new] — [rationale from $ARGUMENTS]. Snapshot: [[_Archive/Snapshots/...]]
136	   ```
137	
138	### For status → closed (archive flow):
139	1. Add final Log entry:
140	   ```
141	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
142	   - CLOSED: [rationale from $ARGUMENTS]. Archived.
143	   ```
144	2. Change frontmatter: `status: closed`
145	3. Move file to `_Archive/TICKER - Company Name.md`:
146	   ```bash
147	   mv "Theses/TICKER - Company Name.md" "_Archive/TICKER - Company Name.md"
148	   ```
149	4. Do NOT rewrite wikilinks across the vault — report expected breakages: `⚠️ [N] notes contain wikilinks to this thesis. Run /lint to review.`
150	
151	## Step 5: Update Sector Note
152	
153	1. Read the Sector Note matching the thesis's `sector:` frontmatter. Search `Sectors/` for a file whose name or `sector:` frontmatter matches. If no matching sector note exists, warn: `⚠️ No sector note found matching sector: [value]. Skipping sector note update. Consider creating a sector note with the Sector Template.` — then skip the rest of Step 5 and proceed to Step 6.
154	2. **For status → active** (e.g., draft→active): Verify the thesis appears in the Active Theses section. If missing, add it.
155	3. **For conviction changes**: If the sector note displays conviction levels alongside thesis links, update to reflect the new level.
156	4. **For status → monitoring**: Keep the thesis in the Active Theses section but annotate as monitoring if the sector note distinguishes.
157	5. **For status → closed**: Remove the thesis from the Active Theses section. If a "Closed/Archived" section exists, add it there. If not, just remove.
158	
159	## Step 6: Update _graph.md (skip if it does not exist)
160	
161	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
162	
163	### For conviction and non-closure status changes:
164	- No graph changes needed (graph tracks links, not conviction/status metadata).
165	
166	### For status → closed:
167	- Remove the archived thesis's entry from the Thesis Adjacency Index
168	- Remove it from all reverse indexes (Macro → Theses, Sector → Theses)
169	- Remove it from Cross-Thesis Clusters
170	- Scan ALL remaining thesis entries in the Adjacency Index and remove the archived ticker from their `cross-thesis:` fields
171	- Move any research notes that were ONLY linked from this thesis to the Orphan list
172	- Update `date:` and `edges:` in frontmatter
173	
174	**Graph validation** (closure only): After all graph edits, re-read the modified section and verify: (1) no unclosed `[[` brackets introduced, (2) `theses:` frontmatter count still within ±2 of actual `Theses/` file count. If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`
175	
176	## Step 7: Update _hot.md
177	
178	Read `_hot.md` then edit (do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive — owned by `/sync`):
179	
180	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /status — [TICKER] [field] [old] → [new]` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.
181	
182	1. **Active Research Thread**: **Same-ticker continuation** — if the current thread already covers the same primary ticker/topic, append a dated line (`YYYY-MM-DD: [update]`) to the existing thread instead of compressing. **New topic**: compress the outgoing thread into a single `*Previous:*` entry (date + one-phrase summary). Write: [TICKER] [field] changed [old] → [new], and the logical next step. Append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.
183	2. **Recent Conviction Changes**: Add entry: `- **[TICKER]**: [field] [old] → [new] — [rationale]`
184	3. **Open Questions**: If closing a thesis, remove any open questions specific to that ticker
185	4. **Portfolio Snapshot**: Update conviction-level counts if conviction changed; update active/monitoring/closed counts if status changed
186	
187	**Word cap**: After all `_hot.md` edits, check total word count. If over 2,000 words, prune `## Sync Archive` entries (oldest first), then `*Previous:*` lines in Active Research Thread (oldest first), until under cap.

192	- **Rationale**: [from $ARGUMENTS]
193	- **Snapshot**: `[[_Archive/Snapshots/...]]` or "skipped (draft→active)"
194	- **Sector Note updated**: [sector name] — [what changed]
195	- **Graph updated**: [edges removed, orphans created] or "no graph changes needed"
196	- **Wikilink breakages** (closure only): `⚠️ [N] notes reference this thesis. Run /lint to review.`

1	---
2	name: stress-test
3	description: Adversarial thesis testing — act as a short seller to find the weakest points in a thesis. Use when user says "stress test", "devil's advocate", "challenge this thesis", or "break [TICKER]".
4	model: opus
5	effort: max
6	allowed-tools: Read Grep Glob Edit Write WebSearch WebFetch Bash(date * defuddle *)
7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly.** No hedge words, no qualifiers, no softening. Dense, specific, evidence-backed.
10	
11	Perform an adversarial stress test on a thesis. Your job is to find reasons this investment FAILS. Do not be balanced — be a prosecutor building the strongest possible case against the position.
12	
13	## Arguments
14	$ARGUMENTS should be a ticker (e.g., "NVDA") or a thesis name. If empty, ask the user which thesis to stress test.
15	
16	## Phase 1: Load the Thesis and All Supporting Evidence
17	1. Read the thesis note from Theses/
18	2. Read EVERY research note linked from the thesis (Related Research + Log entries)
19	3. Read the relevant Sector Note for competitive context
20	4. Read any Macro notes referenced or relevant to the sector
21	5. Grep the vault for the ticker to catch any mentions in other notes the thesis doesn't link to
22	
23	## Phase 2: Internal Contradiction Scan
24	Look specifically for:
25	- Does the Bull Case rely on assumptions that the Bear Case or Risks section already contradicts?
26	- Do research notes contain data points that undermine the Non-consensus Insights?
27	- Has the competitive landscape shifted since the thesis was last updated? (Check research note dates vs thesis Log dates)
28	- Are the Key Metrics internally consistent? (e.g., high revenue growth + high FCF yield is rare — is the company sacrificing one for the other?)
29	- Does the conviction level match the strength of evidence? (High conviction with thin research = red flag)
30	- Are Outstanding Questions still unanswered? If yes, how can conviction be high?
31	- Are existing Conviction Triggers well-defined? Do they cover the actual failure modes, or are the real risks outside the trigger framework? A thesis with vague triggers ("if competition increases") has a hidden vulnerability — it can degrade without ever formally triggering a reassessment.
32	
33	## Phase 3: Build the Short Case
34	Structure as a short seller would pitch to an investment committee:
35	
36	1. **Thesis Vulnerability Summary** — One paragraph: the single biggest reason this investment fails
37	2. **Evidence Against** — 3-5 points, each citing specific data from vault notes:
38	   - Market structure risks the thesis underweights
39	   - Competitive threats documented in sector note but not reflected in thesis
40	   - Macro sensitivities that are unhedged
41	   - Financial assumptions that don't hold under stress
42	   - Management or execution risks
43	3. **Assumption Stress Table**:
44	
45	| Bull Assumption | What Must Be True | Evidence For | Evidence Against | Fragility |
46	|---|---|---|---|---|
47	| [from thesis] | [specific condition] | [cite vault note] | [cite vault note or gap] | 🟢🟡🔴 |
48	
49	4. **Research Gaps** — What does the thesis NOT know that it needs to? Which Outstanding Questions remain unanswered? What data would a serious short seller have that this vault doesn't?
50	5. **Kill Trigger** — What single observable event would invalidate the thesis? Be specific and falsifiable (not "competition increases" but "[COMPANY X] launches [PRODUCT Y] at [PRICE Z] by [DATE]")
51	
52	## Phase 4: Update the Vault
53	1. Save the stress test to `Research/YYYY-MM-DD - [TICKER] - Stress Test.md` with:
54	   ```yaml
55	   ---
56	   date: YYYY-MM-DD
57	   tags: [research, stress-test, TICKER]
58	   sector: [from thesis]
59	   ticker: TICKER
60	   source: vault synthesis
61	   source_type: stress-test
62	   propagated_to: [TICKER]
63	   ---
64	   ```
65	2. Append a Log entry to the thesis note (max 2 lines):
66	   ```
67	   ### YYYY-MM-DD
68	   - Stress test [[Research/YYYY-MM-DD - TICKER - Stress Test]]: [top vulnerability], X/Y assumptions rated 🔴 — conviction [unchanged/weakened: reassess + reason]
69	   ```
70	   > **Drift coupling**: `/sync` Step 3e excludes entries starting with `"Stress test"` from drift detection. Do not change this prefix without updating `/sync`.
71	
72	   Use "weakened" (not "unchanged") when any assumption is rated 🔴 — this ensures `/sync` conviction drift detection counts the entry correctly. The conviction level itself is not changed; the log wording reflects that evidence weakened the case even if the user hasn't acted yet.
73	3. Add the stress test to the thesis's `## Related Research` section (if not already present):
74	   ```
75	   - [[Research/YYYY-MM-DD - TICKER - Stress Test]]
76	   ```
77	4. Do NOT change the conviction level — flag it for the user to decide
78	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
79	
80	5. Update `_graph.md` (skip if it does not exist): add the new stress test research note to the thesis's `research:` list in the Thesis Adjacency Index. Update `date:` in frontmatter.
81	
82	**Graph validation**: After all graph edits, re-read the modified section and verify: (1) no unclosed `[[` brackets introduced, (2) `theses:` frontmatter count still within ±2 of actual `Theses/` file count. If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`
83	
84	## Phase 5: Update _hot.md
85	
86	Read `_hot.md` then edit (do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive — owned by `/sync`):
87	
88	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /stress-test — [TICKER] top vulnerability, conviction [flag status]` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.
89	
90	1. **Active Research Thread**: **Same-ticker continuation** — if the current thread already covers the same primary ticker/topic, append a dated line (`YYYY-MM-DD: [update]`) to the existing thread instead of compressing. **New topic**: compress the outgoing thread into a single `*Previous:*` entry (date + one-phrase summary). Write: stress tested [TICKER], top vulnerability found, and whether conviction reassessment was flagged. Append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.
91	2. **Recent Conviction Changes**: Add entry if conviction reassessment was flagged (note: conviction not changed, flagged for user)
92	3. **Open Questions**: Add any critical research gaps or unanswered questions the stress test exposed
93	
94	**Word cap**: After all `_hot.md` edits, check total word count. If over 2,000 words, prune `## Sync Archive` entries (oldest first), then `*Previous:*` lines in Active Research Thread (oldest first), until under cap.
95	
96	## Phase 6: Report
97	Present findings directly to the user. Lead with the scariest finding. End with a clear verdict: "This thesis survives stress testing" or "This thesis has N critical vulnerabilities that need resolution before conviction can be maintained."
98	
99	**Run `/sync` to propagate stress test findings to affected sector notes and cross-thesis references.**

1	---
2	name: surface
3	description: Surface new insights, potential trades, and research opportunities from existing vault content. Use when user says "surface", "what am I missing", "find opportunities", or "what should I research next".
4	model: opus

7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly.** No hedge words, lead with insights/numbers, tables over prose, every sentence must earn its place.
10	
11	Perform deep insight discovery across the vault. This is the highest-value operation — finding connections and opportunities the user hasn't seen yet.
12	
13	## Scope Resolution
14	
15	Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to determine scope:
16	
17	- **Ticker** (e.g., `NVDA`): Read `_graph.md` and resolve the ticker's adjacency set — its thesis, sector note(s), macro note(s), cross-thesis references, and all linked research. Also include theses that share a sector (one ring outward) for competitive context.
18	- **Sector** (e.g., `semiconductors`): Use `_graph.md` Sector → Theses reverse index to resolve all theses in that sector, plus the sector note, related macro notes, and their linked research.
19	- **No arguments / empty**: Full vault — no scoping (reads everything).
20	
21	If a scope is requested but `_graph.md` does not exist, warn: `⚠️ Graph missing — cannot scope. Run /graph first, or run /surface without arguments for full vault scan.` Then stop.
22	
23	## Phase 1: Portfolio Scan
24	
25	**Scoped mode**: Read only files resolved in Scope Resolution. Skip vault-wide checks that require full portfolio coverage (Attention Allocation ranking, Research Velocity ranking across all theses — these need the full set to be meaningful).
26	
27	**Unscoped mode**: Read all Sector Notes and all active thesis notes (focus on Non-consensus Insights, Risks, and recent Log entries).
28	
29	## Phase 2: Connection Analysis
30	
31	**Portfolio Blind Spots**
32	- Identify sectors or sub-sectors the vault's theses imply are important but have NO coverage. (e.g., if you hold BESI + LITE + NVDA, you're implicitly betting on advanced packaging — do you cover the substrate suppliers? The test equipment vendors? The materials companies?)
33	- Flag value chain nodes that appear in multiple theses' Risk sections but have no dedicated research — these are unmonitored dependencies
34	- Check for "implied but unwritten" theses — tickers mentioned in 3+ research notes that still have no Thesis note. These are ideas the vault has been circling without committing to
35	- Identify adjacent companies that vault research strongly implies should be covered based on competitive dynamics in Sector Notes
36	
37	**Supply Chain Mapping**
38	- Which thesis companies are suppliers, customers, or competitors of each other?
39	- Are there hidden portfolio correlations? (e.g., multiple bets on the same underlying trend)
40	- Would a single event break multiple theses simultaneously?
41	
42	**Macro Exposure Audit**
43	- Map each thesis against current macro scenarios (Iran conflict, AI bubble risk, rate expectations)
44	- Flag theses with unacknowledged macro sensitivity
45	- Identify natural hedges within the portfolio
46	
47	**Catalyst Calendar**
48	- What upcoming events could move multiple positions?
49	- Are there clustered earnings dates that create portfolio risk?
50	- Any regulatory, geopolitical, or technology milestones approaching?
51	
52	**Contrarian Signal Detection**
53	- Where does the vault's thesis directly contradict current sell-side consensus?
54	- Which positions have the widest gap between vault conviction and market pricing?
55	- These are the highest-alpha opportunities — flag them prominently
56	
57	**Research Freshness Audit**
58	- Which theses have the oldest research? (Longest time since last Research/ note)
59	- What developments might have occurred that aren't reflected?
60	- Search the web for 2-3 most material recent developments
61	
62	**Thesis Velocity & Attention Allocation**
63	- **Research velocity ranking**: Which theses received the most research activity recently (new Research/ notes, Log entries, edits)? Which received zero? Rank all active theses by volume of recent activity.
64	- **Attention vs conviction alignment**: Compare where research time was spent against conviction levels. Flag any mismatch — disproportionate time on low-conviction ideas while high-conviction theses go unattended is a resource allocation error.
65	- **Decay alert**: List any active thesis that hasn't been touched (no Log entry, no new linked research) in 30+ days. These are candidates for `/deepen` or `/prune`.
66	
67	## Phase 3: Opportunity Generation
68	
69	Generate 3-5 specific, actionable research prompts ranked by potential conviction impact:
70	
71	For each opportunity:
72	- **Topic**: What to research
73	- **Why now**: What triggered this being relevant
74	- **Vault connection**: Which existing notes it builds on
75	- **Expected impact**: High/Medium/Low potential to change a conviction level
76	- **Suggested approach**: Specific research steps
77	
78	## Phase 4: Output
79	
80	Save findings to:
81	- **Unscoped**: `Research/YYYY-MM-DD - Insight Surface Scan.md`
82	- **Ticker-scoped**: `Research/YYYY-MM-DD - [TICKER] - Surface Scan.md`
83	- **Sector-scoped**: `Research/YYYY-MM-DD - [Sector] - Surface Scan.md`
84	
85	If the file already exists, append a counter (`...2.md`, `...3.md`). Save with:
86	```yaml
87	---
88	date: YYYY-MM-DD
89	tags: [research, meta, surface-scan]
90	status: active
91	source: vault synthesis
92	source_type: synthesis
93	---
94	```
95	
96	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
97	
98	Update `_graph.md` (skip if it does not exist):
99	- Add the surface scan research note to the graph
100	- If new cross-thesis connections were discovered (blind spots, supply chain links, hidden correlations), add them to Cross-Thesis Clusters
101	- If "implied but unwritten" theses were identified, add them to the Orphan list as potential thesis candidates
102	- Update `date:` in frontmatter

If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`

106	Update `_hot.md` (read first, then edit — do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive, owned by `/sync`):

108	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /surface — [scoped/unscoped], top insight` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.

Write: surface scan completed [scoped/unscoped], top insight found, and the logical next research step. Append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.

toolCalls.name: Read
toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	name: sync
3	description: Synchronize recent research learnings across all affected theses, sector notes, and macro documents. Use after a research session or when user says "sync", "propagate", or "update everything".
4	model: opus
5	effort: max
6	allowed-tools: Read Grep Glob Edit Write Bash(date * find * cp * mkdir * touch *)
7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly in all output.** No hedge words, no restating known context, no connective tissue. Every line must contain a data point, an insight, or a specific claim.
10	
11	Propagate recent research insights across all affected vault documents. **This is a deep analytical operation — for each affected note, think through the full implications of new research on every substantive section, not just append links.**
12	
13	## Sync Modes
14	
15	This skill supports three modes based on `$ARGUMENTS`:
16	
17	- **`/sync`** (default) — **Graph-assisted targeted sync.** Uses `_graph.md` to resolve dependencies without brute-force file traversal. Reads only the files that need updating. Performs incremental `_graph.md` update after propagation. Use for routine post-ingest propagation.
18	- **`/sync all`** — **Full brute-force sync.** Reads all theses, all sector notes, all macro notes regardless of graph. Content propagation only — does NOT update `_graph.md` (run `/graph` afterward to rebuild). Use after major research sessions or monthly maintenance.
19	- **`/sync TICKER`** — **Ticker-scoped sync.** Uses `_graph.md` to resolve the ticker's full adjacency set (thesis, sector notes, macro notes, cross-thesis references, linked research). Reads all resolved files and propagates regardless of timestamps. Performs incremental `_graph.md` update. Use after manually editing a thesis or for targeted propagation to a specific area.
20	
21	Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for mode: if "all", run all mode. If a ticker (matches a `Theses/` filename prefix), run ticker-scoped mode. Otherwise, run default graph-assisted mode.
22	
23	## Watermark
24	
25	Change detection uses `.last_sync` (not `_graph.md`) as the timestamp watermark. This decouples the dependency graph from sync timing — accidental edits to `_graph.md` or standalone `/graph` runs cannot cause `/sync` to miss changes.
26	
27	- `.last_sync` is touched at the end of every sync (Step 7.5 — after all writes including graph update)
28	- `/graph` never touches `.last_sync`
29	- `_graph.md` is purely a dependency map — its mtime has no operational meaning for `/sync`
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Step 0: Read and Validate Dependency Graph (default mode only)
34	
35	Read `_graph.md` to load the pre-computed dependency map. This eliminates the need to read 30-50 files just to discover which files are connected.
36	
37	The graph provides:
38	- **Thesis Adjacency Index**: For each thesis — its sector notes, macro notes, research notes, and cross-thesis references
39	- **Reverse Index: Macro → Theses**: Which theses are exposed to each macro note
40	- **Reverse Index: Sector → Theses**: Which theses are in each sector
41	- **Cross-Thesis Clusters**: Bidirectional propagation paths (highest priority)
42	
43	**After reading, validate the graph before trusting it:**
44	1. YAML frontmatter parses without error (all expected fields present: `date`, `theses`, `sectors`, `macro`, `research`, `edges`)
45	2. `## Thesis Adjacency Index` section heading exists
46	3. `## Reverse Index: Macro → Theses` and `## Reverse Index: Sector → Theses` headings exist
47	4. Every `### TICKER - Name` entry in the adjacency index has at least a `sector:` field
48	5. All wikilinks have valid `[[...]]` syntax (no unclosed brackets)
49	6. Frontmatter counts (`theses:`, `sectors:`, `macro:`) are within ±2 of actual file counts in those directories
50	
51	**Fallback triggers:**
52	- `_graph.md` does not exist → fall back to all-mode. Warn: `⚠️ Graph missing — running all-mode. Run /graph afterward to rebuild.`
53	- `date:` is >30 days old → fall back to all-mode. Warn: `⚠️ Graph stale ([N] days old) — running all-mode. Run /graph afterward to rebuild.`
54	- Validation checks 1-3 fail → fall back to all-mode. Warn: `⚠️ Graph structurally corrupt — [specific failure]. Running all-mode. Run /graph to rebuild.`
55	- Validation checks 4-6 fail → proceed with graph-assisted mode but warn: `⚠️ Graph has minor issues — [specific failure]. Sync will proceed but run /graph soon to repair.`
56	
57	## Step 1: Identify Changes
58	
59	- If `$ARGUMENTS` specifies files, use those as the source
60	- Otherwise, use timestamp-based detection to find all files modified since the last sync:
61	  ```bash
62	  find Research/ Theses/ Macro/ Sectors/ -newer .last_sync -name '*.md'
63	  ```
64	  `.last_sync` is the watermark — touched at the end of every sync, so `-newer .last_sync` captures exactly the files changed since the last run. If `.last_sync` does not exist (first run), create it with an epoch timestamp so all files appear changed, then fall back to all mode. Warn: `⚠️ No .last_sync found — first run. Reading all files.`
65	  ```bash
66	  touch -t 197001010000 .last_sync
67	  ```
68	- If no changed files are found and no files were specified, report "Nothing changed since last sync" and stop
69	- Read each modified/new note to extract propagatable insights
70	
71	### Pre-propagation deduplication
72	After reading each changed research note, check its frontmatter for a `propagated_to:` field. This field is set by skills (`/stress-test`, `/scenario`, `/compare`, `/deepen`) that create a research note AND directly append Log entries to thesis notes in the same operation.
73	
74	**Verification pass** — `propagated_to` is not trusted at face value. A skill may fail between writing the research note and completing all thesis updates, leaving tickers claimed as propagated but never actually updated (e.g., `/scenario` affecting 10+ theses, session interruption after ticker 3 of 8). For each ticker listed in `propagated_to:`:
75	
76	1. Locate the thesis file in `Theses/` (match by ticker prefix, e.g., `NVDA` → `Theses/NVDA - *.md`)
77	2. Search the thesis's `## Log` and `## Related Research` sections for the research note's filename (without `.md` extension). For example, if the research note is `Research/2026-04-17 - Scenario - China Taiwan.md`, search for `2026-04-17 - Scenario - China Taiwan`
78	3. **If found** → propagation completed; exclude this research-note → thesis pair from Steps 2–3 (no duplicate Log entry)
79	4. **If NOT found** → propagation failed silently; KEEP this pair in the propagation set so `/sync` completes the missed update. Warn: `⚠️ propagated_to claimed [TICKER] but no Log/Related Research reference found in thesis — re-propagating.`
80	
81	The research note may still propagate to OTHER theses not listed in `propagated_to:` (e.g., cross-thesis references, sector peers).
82	
83	### Graph-assisted mode (default):
84	After identifying changed files, use `_graph.md` to resolve the propagation set:
85	1. For each changed Research note → look up which theses link to it (check Thesis Adjacency Index for any thesis listing that research note)
86	   - **Fallback 1 (new/unlinked notes)**: if no thesis is found in the Adjacency Index, check the research note's `ticker:` frontmatter and outbound `[[Theses/...]]` wikilinks to identify the target thesis directly. Include any matched thesis in the propagation set.
87	   - **Fallback 2 (no ticker, no thesis links)**: if Fallback 1 also yields no match (`ticker:` is absent/empty AND no `[[Theses/...]]` wikilinks exist), apply two additional heuristics:
88	     1. **Tag scan**: Check `tags:` for ticker-like tokens (all-uppercase, 1-5 characters). For each candidate, verify it matches a `Theses/` filename prefix (e.g., tag `NVDA` → `Theses/NVDA - *.md`). Include any matched thesis in the propagation set.
89	     2. **Macro reverse lookup**: Collect all outbound `[[Macro/...]]` wikilinks from the research note. For each, look up the Macro → Theses reverse index in `_graph.md` to resolve affected theses. Include all matched theses in the propagation set.
90	   - If all fallbacks yield no match, log: `ℹ️ [filename] — no propagation target found. Consider adding ticker: frontmatter or [[Theses/...]] wikilinks.` Include in Step 8 report.
91	2. For each changed Thesis → look up its sector notes, macro notes, and cross-thesis references
92	3. For each changed Macro note → look up the Macro reverse index to find affected theses
93	4. For each changed Sector note → look up the Sector reverse index to find member theses
94	5. Union all affected files → this is the set of files to read and potentially update
95	
96	**Missing file resilience**: When reading files in the propagation set, if a file does not exist (e.g., a thesis was archived by `/prune` or `/status` since the graph was last built), skip it gracefully and log: `⚠️ Skipped [filename] — file not found (likely archived). Run /graph to rebuild.` Do NOT fail the sync. Continue processing remaining files. Collect all skipped files and include them in the Step 8 report.
97	
98	### All mode (Two-Pass Triage):
99	
100	Reading all ~40 thesis notes, ~13 sector notes, and ~6 macro notes in full will exceed context limits. Use a lightweight scan to triage by delta significance, then deep-read only notes with meaningful propagation paths.
101	
102	#### Pass 1 — Lightweight scan
103	1. Identify changed files using `.last_sync` watermark (same as default mode). If first run, treat all files as changed.
104	2. For each thesis in Theses/, read **only frontmatter + Summary + last 3 Log entries** (stop reading after the 3rd-most-recent `###` Log heading). This yields ~300-400 words per thesis — manageable for the full set.
105	3. For each sector note in Sectors/, read **only frontmatter + Active Theses + Log sections**.
106	4. Read ALL macro notes in full (small set, ~6 files — always worth full context).
107	
108	#### Pass 2 — Triage and deep read
109	5. Using the lightweight summaries + the changed file list from Pass 1, classify each thesis:
110	   - **High delta**: Directly referenced by a changed research note, shares sector with changed content, has cross-thesis links to changed theses, or its own Log shows recent conviction/status changes → read the **full thesis note**
111	   - **Low delta**: Same macro exposure or adjacent sector to changed content, but no direct reference → read **Summary + Non-consensus Insights + Outstanding Questions + Bull/Bear Case** sections only (skip Business Model, Industry Context, Key Metrics)
112	   - **No delta**: No plausible propagation path from any changed content → do NOT read further. Carry forward the Pass 1 summary for completeness checks only.
113	6. Read sector notes in full **only for sectors containing at least one High-delta thesis**. For other sectors, the Pass 1 scan is sufficient.
114	7. Proceed to Step 2 with the prioritised reading set.
115	
116	This typically reduces deep reads from ~58 files to 15-25 depending on how broadly the changes propagate.
117	
118	### Ticker-scoped mode:
119	1. Read `_graph.md` and locate the ticker's entry in the Thesis Adjacency Index
120	2. Resolve the full adjacency set: the thesis note, all listed sector notes, macro notes, cross-thesis references, and research notes
121	   - **Also scan for unlinked research**: grep `Research/` for files with `ticker: TICKER` in frontmatter not already in the adjacency entry's `research:` list — these are newly ingested notes the graph doesn't know about yet. Include them in the resolved set.
122	3. Read all resolved files — ignore `.last_sync` watermark (this mode is timestamp-independent)
123	4. If `_graph.md` does not exist, warn: `⚠️ Graph missing — cannot resolve ticker scope. Run /graph first, or use /sync (default) instead.` Then stop.
124	5. Proceed to Step 2 with all resolved files as the input set
125	
126	## Step 2: Deep Implication Analysis
127	
128	**This is the critical analytical step. Spend maximum reasoning effort here. Do NOT proceed to writing updates until this analysis is complete for all source notes.**
129	
130	**Source deduplication**: If a thesis appears as a changed source AND one or more Research notes referencing that thesis are also in the changed set, analyze the Research notes as the primary sources. The thesis log entries referencing those Research notes do not contain additional propagatable information — focus analysis effort on the Research notes themselves.
131	
132	For each source note (new/changed research), read it fully and analyse:
133	
134	- **Factual deltas**: New data points, metrics, quotes, events — what is concretely new?
135	- **Assumption challenges**: Which existing thesis assumptions does this validate, weaken, or invalidate? Be specific — name the assumption.
136	- **Second-order implications**: What follows from this information that isn't stated explicitly? (e.g., "TSMC capex cut → ASML order book at risk → BESI utilisation rates drop")
137	- **Competitive dynamics shifts**: Does this change the relative positioning of companies within a sector?
138	- **Macro linkages**: Does this micro-level data have macro implications, or vice versa?
139	
140	The quality of the entire propagation depends on the depth of thinking in this step.
141	
142	## Step 3: Update Thesis Notes
143	
144	For each affected thesis:
145	
146	### 3a: Read the Full Thesis
147	Read the entire thesis note. Understand the current state of every section before proposing changes.
148	
149	### 3b: Analyse Impact Per Section
150	Map the insights from Step 2 against each thesis section. For each, determine: does the new research provide a meaningful delta? Be selective — not every piece of research affects every section.
151	
152	**Section-by-section analysis framework:**
153	
154	| Section | Update when... |
155	|---|---|
156	| **Summary** | The core investment case has shifted — not incremental data, but a change in the thesis direction or key assumption |
157	| **Non-consensus Insights** | New evidence strengthens/weakens an existing insight, OR a genuinely new non-consensus angle emerged |
158	| **Outstanding Questions** | A question has been answered (mark resolved with answer + date), OR the research raises new questions worth tracking |
159	| **Business Model** | Structural change to revenue model, product mix, go-to-market, or segment economics |
160	| **Industry Context** | Competitive positioning, market share, or pricing power shifted |
161	| **Bull Case** | New supporting evidence, or a bull scenario element was validated/invalidated |
162	| **Bear Case** | New counter-evidence, or a bear scenario element was validated/invalidated |
163	| **Catalysts** | New catalyst identified, or existing catalyst resolved — mark outcome |
164	| **Risks** | New risk emerged, existing risk probability changed materially, or a risk was retired |
165	| **Conviction Triggers** | New evidence validates/invalidates a trigger condition, OR a trigger threshold needs updating based on changed fundamentals |
166	
167	**Do NOT update sections where the delta is trivial or where you'd be restating what the research note already says. The thesis contains synthesised conclusions, not duplicated research content.**
168	
169	### 3c: Pre-Edit Safety — Snapshot
170	
171	Classify proposed edits by tier:
172	
173	**Tier A — Snapshot required:** Editing or rewriting existing text in Summary, Non-consensus Insights, Outstanding Questions, Business Model, Industry Context, Bull Case, Bear Case, Conviction Triggers.
174	
175	**Tier B — No snapshot needed:** Appending new Catalysts, Risks, or Conviction Triggers. Appending to Log, Related Research. Updating numbers in Key Metrics table.
176	
177	**If any Tier A edits are planned for a thesis:**
178	1. Create snapshot directory if needed:
179	   ```bash
180	   mkdir -p _Archive/Snapshots
181	   ```
182	2. Copy the current thesis note (preserving the original at its path so all wikilinks remain intact):
183	   ```bash
184	   cp "Theses/TICKER - Company Name.md" "_Archive/Snapshots/TICKER - Company Name (pre-sync YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM).md"
185	   ```
186	3. Read the newly created snapshot, then add to its frontmatter:
187	   ```yaml
188	   snapshot_of: "[[Theses/TICKER - Company Name]]"
189	   snapshot_date: YYYY-MM-DD
190	   snapshot_trigger: sync
191	   snapshot_batch: sync-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
192	   ```
193	   > **Batch ID**: Generate the `HHMM` timestamp once at the start of the snapshot phase (e.g., via `date +%H%M`). Reuse the same `snapshot_batch` value across ALL snapshots created in this sync run (thesis, sector, macro). This enables `/rollback` cascade detection to distinguish same-day repeat operations.
194	
195	4. Proceed with edits to the ORIGINAL thesis note.
196	
197	### 3d: Apply Substantive Edits
198	For each section identified in 3b, make precise edits:
199	
200	- **Integrate, don't append** — weave new evidence into existing prose so the section reads as a coherent, up-to-date analysis. Do NOT add "Update (2026-04-16): ..." blocks within sections.
201	- **Preserve voice and structure** — match the existing writing style and analytical depth of the section.
202	- **Net deletions are OK** — if new evidence invalidates a prior claim, remove the claim rather than leaving it alongside its contradiction.
203	- **Mark resolved Outstanding Questions** — format: ~~Original question~~ → Resolved YYYY-MM-DD: [answer]. Keep resolved questions visible so the analytical trail is preserved.
204	
205	### 3e: Mechanical Updates (every time)
206	- **Key Metrics**: Update table if new numbers are available
207	- **Related Research**: Add wikilink to new research note
208	- **Conviction trigger check**: If new data satisfies a pre-defined Conviction Trigger condition, flag it:
209	  `⚡ Trigger hit: [quote the trigger] — [what happened]`
210	- **Conviction drift detection**:
211	  > **Log Format Contract** — drift detection depends on exact prefix matching across skills. Changing any prefix in the source skill breaks detection here. Update both locations together.
212	  > | Prefix | Source | Drift role |
213	  > |---|---|---|
214	  > | `"Stress test"` | `/stress-test` Phase 4 step 2 | Excluded from drift window |
215	  > | `"Deepened"` / `"↳ CORRECTION: Deepened"` | `/deepen` Phase 5c | Excluded when ≤1 day after a stress test |
216	  > | `"Conviction reaffirmed"` | `/status` Step 2R | Anchors (resets) drift window |
217	  > | `"Status change: conviction"` | `/status` Step 4 | Anchors (resets) drift window |
218	
219	  Scan Log entries backward from most recent, **excluding entries that begin with "Stress test"** (these are periodic adversarial reviews, not evidence of fundamental deterioration — counting them inflates drift signals). **Also exclude entries that begin with "Deepened" or "↳ CORRECTION: Deepened" when they occur within 1 calendar day of a "Stress test" entry for the same ticker** — these are gap-filling research triggered by the stress test (chain §3g), not independent evidence of deterioration. If a "Conviction reaffirmed" or "Status change: conviction" entry is found, anchor the window there — only count entries AFTER the anchor. If fewer than 3 entries exist after the anchor, drift detection has insufficient data and does not fire. If no anchor exists, use the last 5 entries as the window.
220	  - conviction: high but 3+ entries in window weakening → `⚠️ Conviction drift — [N]/[window size] recent updates flagged headwinds. Reassess. To acknowledge after review: /status TICKER reaffirm [rationale]`
221	  - conviction: low but 3+ entries in window strengthening → `📈 Positive drift — [N]/[window size] recent updates supportive. Reassess. To acknowledge after review: /status TICKER reaffirm [rationale]`
222	
223	### 3f: Log Entry (last — summarises everything above)
224	
225	**Retry deduplication**: Before appending, scan the thesis's last 3 `### YYYY-MM-DD` Log heading blocks for an entry matching BOTH today's date AND the same source research note wikilink (`[[Research/...]]`). If a match is found, this thesis was already updated by a prior `/sync` run that failed before completing all propagation — skip the Log append for this thesis to avoid duplicates. Log: `ℹ️ Skipped duplicate Log entry for [TICKER] — already contains [date] + [[research-note]].` Include skipped theses in the Step 8 report under the **Deduplication skips** line.
226	
227	Append to Log section:
228	```
229	### YYYY-MM-DD
230	- [[Research/source-note]]: [what changed] — [conviction impact: unchanged/strengthened/weakened + 1 reason]
231	```
232	Max 2 lines. The log is an audit trail — the analysis lives in the updated sections above.
233	
234	## Step 4: Update Sector Notes
235	
236	For each affected Sector Note:
237	
238	### 4a: Pre-Edit Safety — Snapshot
239	If edits will modify existing analytical text (competitive dynamics, value chain analysis, sector-level observations, company comparison narratives) — not just adding links or new research entries:
240	1. ```bash
241	   cp "Sectors/Sector Name.md" "_Archive/Snapshots/Sector Name (pre-sync YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM).md"
242	   ```
243	2. Read the newly created snapshot, then add to its frontmatter:
244	   ```yaml
245	   snapshot_of: "[[Sectors/Sector Name]]"
246	   snapshot_date: YYYY-MM-DD
247	   snapshot_trigger: sync
248	   snapshot_batch: sync-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
249	   ```
250	   Reuse the batch ID generated in Step 3c.
251	
252	### 4b: Apply Updates
253	- **Add new research notes** to the research listing
254	- **Update competitive dynamics** if the research changes relative positioning of companies in the sector
255	- **Update value chain analysis** if supply chain relationships shifted
256	- **Revise sector-level observations** if cross-company patterns emerged from the new research
257	- **Update company comparison tables** with new data points if available
258	
259	Skip snapshot if only adding research note links to the listing.
260	
261	## Step 5: Update Macro Notes
262	
263	For each affected Macro note:
264	
265	### 5a: Pre-Edit Safety — Snapshot
266	If edits will modify existing analytical text (scenario analysis, probability weightings, trading/allocation implications) — not just adding wikilinks:
267	1. ```bash
268	   cp "Macro/Note Name.md" "_Archive/Snapshots/Note Name (pre-sync YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM).md"
269	   ```
270	2. Read the newly created snapshot, then add to its frontmatter:
271	   ```yaml
272	   snapshot_of: "[[Macro/Note Name]]"
273	   snapshot_date: YYYY-MM-DD
274	   snapshot_trigger: sync
275	   snapshot_batch: sync-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
276	   ```
277	   Reuse the batch ID generated in Step 3c.
278	
279	### 5b: Apply Updates
280	- **Assess macro-micro linkage**: Does the new micro-level research validate, weaken, or complicate the macro framework?
281	- **Update scenario analysis** if new data changes probability weightings
282	- **Add wikilinks** to the new research
283	- **Revise trading/allocation implications** if the macro thesis has shifted based on new evidence
284	
285	Skip snapshot if only adding wikilinks to the research.
286	
287	## Step 6: Update _hot.md
288	
289	> **Chain finalization (_hot.md)**: If `## Session Chain` shows active entries from a workflow chain, the Active Research Thread already contains short per-step lines from prior skills. Apply this sync's update as a full entry (normal compress/Previous logic), consolidating the chain's work into a final thread state. The Session Chain section itself is cleared in Step 7.
290	
291	If `_hot.md` does not exist (first run), create it with sections: `## Active Research Thread`, `## Latest Sync`, `## Sync Archive`, `## Recent Conviction Changes`, `## Open Questions`, `## Portfolio Snapshot`, `## Session Chain` (set to `*No active chain.*`). Use frontmatter: `date: YYYY-MM-DD`, `tags: [meta, hot-cache]`.
292	
293	**Lifecycle rules — enforce strictly:**
294	1. **Update the date** in frontmatter and heading
295	2. **Active Research Thread**: **Same-ticker continuation** — if the current thread already covers the same primary ticker/topic, append a dated line (`YYYY-MM-DD: [update]`) to the existing thread instead of compressing. **New topic**: compress the outgoing thread into a single `*Previous:*` entry (date + one-phrase summary). Write the new thread (current focus + next step), then append `*Previous:*` line(s) — max 5, drop oldest.
296	3. **Latest Sync**: Replace the existing Latest Sync section entirely with this sync's summary. One bullet per thesis updated, max 1 line each. Format:
297	   ```
298	   - **[TICKER]**: [what changed] — [conviction impact]
299	   ```
300	4. **Previous Syncs**: Compress the outgoing Latest Sync into a single summary line and prepend it to a "## Sync Archive" section. Keep max 3 archived entries. Delete oldest if over 3.
301	5. **Open Questions**: If any Outstanding Questions were resolved on theses during this sync (Step 3d), mark them resolved in _hot.md's Open Questions. If conviction triggers or drift were flagged (Step 3e), add the reassessment question.
302	6. **Recent Conviction Changes**: If any conviction triggers hit (Step 3e ⚡) or drift was flagged (Step 3e ⚠️/📈), add an entry noting the flag (not the change itself — conviction changes require `/status`).
303	7. **Hard cap**: After writing, check total word count. If _hot.md exceeds 2,000 words, prune Sync Archive entries (oldest first) until under the cap.
304	
305	## Step 7: Incremental Graph Update (default mode only)
306	
307	> **Chain finalization (graph — default/ticker mode)**: After completing the incremental graph update, clear `## Session Chain` in `_hot.md` to `*No active chain.*` **and remove any Graph Debt line**. The sync's incremental update naturally captures all graph changes deferred by prior chain steps (new files/links detected from vault state). This makes `/sync` the natural endpoint for any workflow chain.
308	
309	**Skip this step entirely in all mode** — but **do reset `_graph.md`'s `date:` frontmatter to `1970-01-01`** if the file exists. This forces the next default `/sync` into all-mode fallback (date >30 days), preventing it from relying on a now-stale graph. All-mode runs should still be followed by `/graph` for a full rebuild — incremental updates after touching 30+ files would produce an unreliable graph.
310	
311	**All-mode Graph Debt preservation**: Before resetting the graph date, read `## Session Chain` in `_hot.md`. If an active chain exists with `Graph deferred: [N]` where N > 0:
312	1. Warn in the Step 8 report:
313	   ```
314	   ⚠️ Active chain had [N] deferred graph changes. /sync all cannot capture them.
315	   Converting to Graph Debt. Run /graph to resolve.
316	   ```
317	2. Clear the active chain steps but **preserve as Graph Debt**:
318	   ```
319	   *No active chain.*
320	   **⚠️ Graph debt**: [N] deferred from [YYYY-MM-DD] (/skill1, /skill2). Run `/graph` to capture.
321	   ```
322	   Extract the date and skill names from the cleared chain's `Steps` and `Date` fields. If Graph Debt already exists from a prior chain, accumulate: add the new deferred count to the existing debt count and note both dates.
323	
324	If the chain has `Graph deferred: 0` (or no active chain), clear normally to `*No active chain.*` — but **preserve any existing Graph Debt line** (all-mode does not resolve debt since it skips the incremental graph update).
325	
326	For each file created or modified during this sync:
327	1. **New research note created** → Add it to the relevant thesis's `research:` list in the Thesis Adjacency Index
328	2. **New wikilink added to a thesis** → Add the target to the appropriate field (cross-thesis, sector, macro, or research). **Reverse index**: if the added link is a sector or macro note, also add the thesis ticker to that note's row in the corresponding reverse index table
329	3. **New wikilink removed from a thesis** → Remove the target from the appropriate field. **Reverse index**: if the removed link is a sector or macro note, also remove the thesis ticker from that note's row in the corresponding reverse index table
330	4. **New thesis created** → Add a full adjacency entry with all fields populated. **Reverse index**: add the thesis ticker to every sector and macro row it references
331	5. **Thesis archived/deleted** → Remove its adjacency entry and remove it from all reverse indexes and cluster tables
332	6. **New cross-thesis connection discovered** → Update the Cross-Thesis Clusters table if it represents a new cluster or extends an existing one
333	7. **Orphan status changed** → If a previously orphan research note was linked to a thesis, remove it from the Orphan list
334	
335	Update the frontmatter:
336	- `date:` → today's date
337	- `edges:` → increment/decrement based on links added/removed (estimate is fine)
338	
339	### Post-write validation
340	After completing all graph edits, re-read `_graph.md` and verify:
341	1. YAML frontmatter still parses without error
342	2. All three section headings still present (Thesis Adjacency Index, both Reverse Indexes)
343	3. No unclosed `[[` wikilink brackets introduced by the edits
344	4. The adjacency entry count matches the `theses:` frontmatter value (±2)
345	
346	If any check fails, warn: `⚠️ Graph may have been corrupted by this sync's incremental update — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.` Include this warning in the Step 8 report.
347	
348	## Step 7.5: Touch Watermark
349	
350	**Mandatory. Runs after ALL writes are complete (content + graph), immediately before reporting.**
351	
352	```bash
353	touch .last_sync
354	```
355	
356	This is the last mutation in the sync. If any prior step fails or the session is interrupted, the watermark remains unset and the next `/sync` will re-process the same changes — safe by default.
357	
358	## Step 8: Report
359	
360	- **Mode**: default (graph-assisted) | all
361	- **Files read**: [count]
362	- **Files updated**: [count]
363	- **Snapshots created**: [list with paths, or "none"]
364	- **Thesis updates** (one per thesis):
365	  - [TICKER]: [sections modified] — [conviction impact]. Snapshot: `[[_Archive/Snapshots/...]]` or N/A
366	- **Sector Note updates**: [list with one-line summary each]
367	- **Macro note updates**: [list with one-line summary each]
368	- **Propagation recovery**: [list any tickers where `propagated_to` was claimed but verification found no Log/Related Research entry — these were re-propagated by this sync. If none, omit this line.]
369	- **Deduplication skips**: [list any tickers where a Log entry was skipped because the same date + research note wikilink already existed from a prior partial sync. If none, omit this line.]
370	- **Unresolved research notes**: [list any research notes where all fallbacks (graph, ticker/thesis links, tags, macro reverse index) yielded no propagation target. If none, omit this line.]
371	- Conviction triggers hit: [list any]
372	- Conviction drift flags raised: [list any]
373	- New connections discovered: [any surprising links]
374	- Graph updates (default mode): [edges added/removed, orphans resolved]
375	- **All-mode only**: `⚠️ Graph not updated. Run /graph to rebuild.`
376	- **All-mode Graph Debt** (if triggered): `⚠️ [N] deferred graph changes converted to Graph Debt. Run /graph to resolve.`
377

1	---
2	name: thesis
3	description: Create a new investment thesis note with full analysis. Use when user says "create thesis", "new thesis", or "build a case for TICKER"
4	model: opus
5	effort: max
6	allowed-tools: Read Grep Glob Edit Write WebSearch WebFetch Bash(date * defuddle *)
7	---
8	
9	**Follow CLAUDE.md Writing Standards strictly.** No hedge words, lead with insights/numbers, tables over prose, every sentence must earn its place.
10	
11	Create a comprehensive thesis note for $ARGUMENTS.
12	
13	## Step 1: Duplicate Check
14	Before creating anything, search the vault for existing notes on this ticker/topic:
15	- Grep Theses/ for the ticker
16	- Grep Research/ for the ticker
17	- Grep _Archive/ for the ticker
18	- If an active thesis already exists in Theses/, stop and tell the user. Suggest `/deepen` instead.
19	- If an archived thesis is found in _Archive/, warn: `⚠️ Archived thesis found: [[_Archive/TICKER - Company Name]]. Review the closure rationale before recreating. Use /rollback to restore the previous thesis instead?` Wait for user confirmation before proceeding.
20	
21	## Step 2: Vault Research
22	- Read the relevant Sector Note to understand existing coverage and competitive context
23	- Search /Research and /Macro for any notes mentioning this ticker or topic
24	- Read related thesis notes for competitive dynamics and cross-thesis connections
25	
26	## Step 3: Web Research
27	- Search for recent earnings results, analyst consensus, key financials
28	- Search for competitive dynamics, market share data, industry trends
29	- Search for bear cases and risks — actively look for reasons NOT to invest
30	- Search for sell-side consensus to identify where a non-consensus view could exist
31	
32	## Step 4: Create the Thesis
33	Save to `Theses/TICKER - Company Name.md` with:
34	
35	```yaml
36	---
37	date: [today's date]
38	tags: [thesis, SECTOR-TAG, TICKER]
39	status: draft
40	conviction: [high|medium|low — assess honestly]
41	sector: [primary sector]
42	ticker: TICKER
43	source: [primary source URL or description]
44	---
45	```
46	
47	### Required Sections (all 13, in this order):
48	
49	1. **Summary** — One paragraph investment case. Lead with the non-consensus angle, not a business description.
50	
51	2. **Key Non-consensus Insights** — 3-5 one-paragraph summaries of perspectives foreign to the mainstream narrative. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT SECTION. Think deeply about what the market is missing — technological shifts, management/culture edges, competitive dynamics, investor biases, business model transitions.
52	
53	3. **Outstanding Questions** — 3-10 one-paragraph summaries of what a skeptical investment committee would ask before approving. These should be genuinely hard questions, not softballs. Each should identify what data or event would answer it.
54	
55	4. **Business Model & Product Description** — In-depth breakdown of the business model with analogies/comparisons to make it intuitive. Key revenue/profit-generating products with technical specs where relevant. Revenue segmentation by reported segment or a novel heuristic that better captures the economics.
56	
57	5. **Industry Context** — Competitive dynamics, market share and pricing power shifts, value chain analysis. Where does this company sit in the value chain? Who has leverage over whom? What structural forces are reshaping the industry?
58	
59	6. **Key Metrics** — Table format:
60	
61	| Metric | Value | Notes |
62	|--------|-------|-------|
63	| Market Cap | | |
64	| EV/Revenue | | |
65	| Revenue Growth | | |
66	| Gross Margin | | |
67	| FCF Yield | | |
68	
69	7. **Bull Case** — Explicit upside scenario with specific drivers. What has to go right? Include valuation target or framework if possible.
70	
71	8. **Bear Case** — Explicit downside scenario with specific drivers. Be genuinely adversarial — don't softball this. What does the world look like where this investment loses money?
72	
73	9. **Catalysts** — Near-term events with approximate dates that could move the stock. Include both positive and negative catalysts.
74	
75	10. **Risks** — What breaks the thesis. Minimum 3 risks. Distinguish between thesis risks (the investment case is wrong) and position risks (the thesis is right but the stock goes down anyway).
76	
77	11. **Conviction Triggers** — Pre-defined, falsifiable if/then statements that govern conviction changes:
78	    ```
79	    → HIGH if: [specific observable condition]
80	    → LOW if: [specific observable condition]
81	    → CLOSE if: [specific observable condition]
82	    ```
83	    These must be concrete and testable, not vague ("if competition increases"). Good example: "→ LOW if TSMC cuts capex guidance by >10% in next 2 quarters."
84	
85	12. **Related Research** — Wikilinks to all relevant vault notes (research, sector notes, macro notes, related theses).
86	
87	13. **Log** — Initial entry:
88	    ```
89	    ### YYYY-MM-DD
90	    - Initial thesis created. Conviction: [level] — [one-line rationale]
91	    ```
92	
93	## Step 5: Update the Vault
94	- **Sector Note update**: Search `Sectors/` for a file matching the thesis's `sector:` frontmatter. If no matching sector note exists, warn: `⚠️ No sector note found matching sector: [value]. Skipping sector note update. Consider creating a sector note with the Sector Template.` and skip to the next bullet. **If status is `active`**: add the thesis to the appropriate Sector Note "Active Theses" section. **If status is `draft`**: skip — the thesis will be added when promoted via `/status TICKER status draft→active`
95	- Add wikilinks from any existing Research notes that mention this ticker
96	
97	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain, SKIP graph update below and increment `Graph deferred`. If starting or no chain, proceed.
98	
99	- Update `_graph.md` (skip if it does not exist): add a full adjacency entry for the new thesis with all fields (sector notes, macro notes, research notes, cross-thesis references)
100	
101	**Graph validation**: After all graph edits, re-read the modified section and verify: (1) no unclosed `[[` brackets introduced, (2) `theses:` frontmatter count still within ±2 of actual `Theses/` file count. If either check fails: `⚠️ Graph may be corrupted — [specific failure]. Run /graph to rebuild.`
102	
103	## Step 6: Update _hot.md
104	
105	Read `_hot.md` then edit (do NOT touch Latest Sync or Sync Archive — owned by `/sync`):
106	
107	> **Chain-aware**: Per CLAUDE.md Session Chain Protocol — if joining an active chain: append `YYYY-MM-DD: /thesis — [TICKER] created, conviction [level]` to Active Research Thread only (skip compress/Previous), mark step ✅, then proceed to remaining sections. If starting or no chain, set Session Chain and apply full update below.
